qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Consolidate 5 independent /allow grant-read implementations into lib/allowlist.py

out_of_scope_observations.observation: IS_SUBAGENT firewall test for git-guard only verifies _check_git_allowlist returns False; does not monkeypatch read_grant_for_git_command/consume_grant_for_posttool to assert they are not called. Bash and posttool firewall paths are not directly tested for 'no lib call/no delete'. Implementation line order is correct, so this is not a functional blocker.

Path traversal via sid: no sanitization on session_id before f-string interpolation into /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-{sid}.json. A sid containing '/' could produce a path outside /tmp. However, Claude session IDs come from Claude's own runtime (UUIDs), not user-supplied input. No real exploit vector exists in the current threat model. Out of scope per cycle requirements.

Codex (iteration 2) flagged that the word 'Subagent' (mixed-case) still appears in docstrings at lines 9, 10, 152, 177 after the IS_SUBAGENT token removal. AC8 enumerates three specific forbidden tokens: 'agent_id', 'is_subagent_context', 'IS_SUBAGENT'. None of those exact tokens are present. 'Subagent' (mixed-case) is plain-English description matching the intent clause 'the library has no subagent awareness' and is not one of the three enumerated forbidden identifiers. Codex finding classified as out_of_scope nitpick: AC8 literal text does not forbid the word 'subagent'. Documented for future BA awareness if intent is to forbid the word entirely.

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: 5 independent open/flock/JSON-load/match implementations in 4 callers have been replaced with calls to lib/allowlist.py canonical functions. posttool-allowlist-consume.py, pretool-git-privilege-guard.py, pretool-bash-safety.sh (heredoc shim), and pretool-write-guard.sh all delegate to the library. All IS_SUBAGENT firewalls remain in callers. os.unlink confined to consume_grant_for_posttool. Force-push ordering invariant preserved.
qa.overall_assessment: Iteration 2 PASS. Both prior-iteration failures are resolved. AC1: grep for 'candidates: list,' returns 0 matches — both signatures at lines 40 and 99 now read 'candidates: list[str],'. AC8: grep for IS_SUBAGENT returns empty — all 3 token occurrences removed from allowlist.py. Python syntax valid. 25/25 unit tests still pass. All 9 previously-passing ACs (AC2-AC7, AC9-AC11) show no regression. Codex adversarial review raised one finding (residual 'Subagent' word in comments) which is classified out_of_scope: AC8 enumerates three specific identifiers and none are present; the plain-English word 'Subagent' is not among them.

generated_tests.description: 25 unit tests covering _match_loaded_grant, _load_and_match, read_grant, read_grant_for_git_command, match_grant_for_bash_command, consume_grant_for_posttool. Includes AC8 IS_SUBAGENT firewall scenarios.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 1 ISSUE and 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY. ISSUE: 'Subagent' (mixed-case) still appears in lines 9, 10, 152, 177 of allowlist.py — if AC8 means 'no subagent awareness' literally rather than 'no exact token', PASS may be vulnerable. PROPOSED_FIX: reword docstrings to avoid 'subagent' entirely. OBSERVATION_ONLY: Python 3.9 compat is not a blocker; file requires Python 3.12+.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: ISSUE classified as out_of_scope nitpick. AC8 enumerates exactly three forbidden identifiers: agent_id, is_subagent_context, IS_SUBAGENT. None present. The word 'Subagent' (mixed-case) is plain-English description of the intent clause and is not among the enumerated tokens. Codex's proposed fix would require an additional change beyond the literal AC text. Finding documented in out_of_scope_observations[2]. OBSERVATION_ONLY: acknowledged, no action required.
